module_mood_contract: learning through contrast — 'I can feel what packaging adds now; I couldn't have read it as a lecture'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honoured at every teaching beat.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 1 (worktree setup) is a setup beat — mood-exempt per carve-out (mood_score: null). Phase 2 (lectures + exercise) mood_score 8: contrast lands when M4 artifact is available; Start-here body avoids pre-announcing the exercise's payoff. Phase 3 (Key Concepts + re-send + closing lecture) mood_score 9: packaging feels earned, closing lecture lands as recognition not repetition — 'three-pattern' edit in Key Concepts prevents pre-emption. Close mood_score 9: stakes line reads peer-flat, module closes on contrast in progress.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood — unease is not-knowing, not having-done-wrong.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Student-facing body frames M4 run as 'supposed to underdeliver' — data, not failure. Key Concepts: 'The three failure modes earn their names by reading them in your own artefact, not from a slide' frames discovery, not correction. Maintainer block names diagnosis-as-blame as failure mode and provides escape hatch.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea leads with positive disciplines: reading through lenses, building validation, assembling reference, re-sending packaged. Key Concepts bullets all lead with discipline verbs: earning names, one move per failure mode, pickable verifier shapes, same-task-twice, artefact rules. No failure word headlines any section.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — no front-running of downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts bullet edited from 'The three-pattern is one move per failure mode' to 'One move per failure mode, not three to memorise' — removes the Ronacher label before the closing lecture earns it. No verifier-shape names, plan.md vocabulary, or M6 team-kit concepts appear in student body. Pre-read addition (Clean Code Is Steering) is at Prework, not body, and does not name the three-pattern.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Six bullets map to AE101 themes: 'earn their names by reading your own artefact' (capture-your-world + 90%-correct); 'one move per failure mode' (compounding by subtraction); 'verifier shapes are pickable' (mirror + compounding); 'same task run twice with packaging as the only changed variable IS the lesson' (compounding + non-agentic-default); 'artefact rules, self-reports don't' (90%-correct); 'your verifier is your first eval' (compounding → team infrastructure). Three or more themes present across six bullets.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — gate-then-decorate, breadcrumbs on survivors only.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All six bullets lead with discipline per §3, none front-run downstream modules per §4. One breadcrumb present: 'Remember: the artefact rules, self-reports don't.' on the 90%-correct/self-awareness bullet. No standalone Themes section, no rule-index stamping, engineer register throughout.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: What You'll Learn shape — capability register, strategic theme verbs lead.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Five bullets: Diagnose / Pair / Build / Assemble / Re-send. Strategic theme verbs lead. No Bloom-tag parentheticals, no mechanism tails, no author-defence asides. 'Module 6 gives evals their full weight' forward-arc tail on last bullet is exempt per carve-out. Each bullet names a concrete artifact or named move in engineer register.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture placement verified: (1) reading-the-return.md — shared at M4 Debrief close (pre-read, not in this module's body); (2) learning-through-contrast.md — Start here opener, arms the student to diagnose (vocabulary/arm-to-do type); (3) what-packaging-is.md — placed at line 64, after the full exercise + re-send block. This is the meta-frame closer naming Ronacher's three-pattern from felt evidence. Correct placement per rule.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Two lectures before the exercise: reading-the-return (pre-read, not in-module dosage) and learning-through-contrast. The latter frames diagnostic stance and lenses without naming practitioner terms or explaining tradeoffs — Ronacher/Cherny/Intercom names reserved for the closer per check_lectures.md §2. Dosage is within 'spirit + diagnostic lenses + one operational primitive' maximum for contrast-mood openers.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Two load-bearing forcing functions are inside registry markers: {{prompt:ae101-m5-worktree-setup}} at line 30 and {{prompt:ae101-m5-rerun-packaged}} at line 58. Body prose around both prompts is descriptive of what and why (appropriate), not the forcing function itself. The post-re-send body note (lines 60-62: 'If the summary reads polished, ask the agent...') is an optional recovery move — within the carve-out for optional refinement moves.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; no authored conversation or pause cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'share with your neighbor', 'tell the room', 'take 90 seconds and compare', or room-management cues in student-facing body. Long re-send wait is a natural pause anchor with no authored cue. Power-settings callout ('laptop stays awake and plugged in') is operational instruction about the work, not cohort management.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Plug points + UX — pluggable and operable.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Plug points in maintainer block name concrete integration commitments: M4 artefact location, sponsor-stated rules-file home, sponsor-stated test/CI conventions. Structural invitation: Phase 3 verifier shape integrates with sponsor CI gate. Fast-operator path: two registry-sourced prompts → exercise file (linked) → re-send prompt is coherent without reading body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Leap test in maintainer block names three falsifiable outcomes with named verbs and falsification conditions: (1) reads multi-hour artefact through three named lenses + quotes specific moments; (2) verifier file at stable path naming failure mode and check-shape; (3) two distinct runs of same scoped task (un-packaged baseline + packaged re-run). Carve-out for arc-mood (M5 rescue) applied — tests name artifacts student now has, not activities.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch for every forcing function.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Full per-phase failure mode + escape hatch table present in maintainer block. Eight rows covering: worktree setup (Connections blocker), Phase 1 diagnosis-as-blame, Phase 1 over-diagnosis, Phase 2 prescription-skip, Phase 3 verifier-shape mismatch, Phase 4 reference-as-rewrite, Debrief re-send anxiety, Closing lecture pre-empt. Each row names forcing function, dominant failure, and specific recovery move.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Worktree-setup marker preceded by functional direction (open the session, ask it). Rerun-packaged marker preceded by 'Ask Claude to re-run the same Module 4 task using the reference, plan.md, and verifier you just built.' — command verb, semantic summary, under 20 words.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Prompts live in registry, referenced by marker.
rules_evaluated.evidence: {{prompt:ae101-m5-worktree-setup}} at line 30 and {{prompt:ae101-m5-rerun-packaged}} at line 58 — registry markers with lead-in sentences present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Git-output greps that assert exact-N need a multi-result fallback.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 32-33: body callout immediately after the worktree-setup marker names the multi-result case and provides fallback: filter by 'M4 starting point' log message; fall back to SHA from transcript if commit message was rewritten. Option A shape (body callout after prompt) for engineer-audience AE101.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands — 8+/10 at every teaching beat.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 1: null (setup beat, exempt). Phase 2: 8 (contrast lands; slight drag from thin-artifact edge case, resolved by trainer). Phase 3: 9 (packaging earned, closing lecture recognition beat). Close: 9. No teaching beat below 8.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — contrast stays live at M5 close.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Re-send is sent but not read within M5. Closing lecture names the three-pattern but the second run is still executing. 'Bring to Module 6' defers the read explicitly. The two-run arc stays open; M6 is where the contrast is read.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands — same-task-twice contrast reliably triggers across persona variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Teaching moment is structurally enforced by the two-run arc built across M4 and M5. For the modal persona (M4 artifact accessible), the contrast fires reliably. The thin-artifact edge case has a maintainer-block escape hatch; it does not prevent the modal teaching moment from landing. No persona variation tested could skip the two-run structure.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — module leads with and honours the Big Idea.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea: 'Read the un-packaged Module 4 run through three failure-mode lenses, build the validation that would have caught each, assemble the reference and plan.md, and re-send the same task packaged. The contrast is the lesson.' Module structure matches exactly: diagnose → build verifier → assemble reference+plan.md → re-send packaged. Every section honours this framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off to M6 open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 68-70: 'Module 6 reads the packaged run. Subtler misses, subtler drift. Your verifier gets its full name as an eval, and the team kit takes its first inherited skill.' Specific and matches M6 opening framing. Pre-reads before M6 (Klaassen verifier article) primes two M6 teaching moments (reliability as a number, verifier-as-panel).
